jobs.purpose: Dispatched on tenancy::TenantProvisioned. Creates the tenant's default 10-entry age-group catalog from `data/default-age-groups.json`. Idempotent — a second dispatch for the same tenant no-ops.
steps: Resolve tenant + verify no existing seeded AgeGroup rows (idempotency check by (tenant_id, is_seeded=true)).
Parse `data/default-age-groups.json` — 10 entries: U6, U8, U10, U12, U14, U16, U18, Adult (is_default=true), Senior, Masters.
For each entry: invoke AgeGroupProvisioner with { tenant, is_seeded=true, sport_key=null, organization_id=null, ...entry }.
The AgeGroupProvisioner skips age_group_slot consumption for seeded rows (is_seeded=true bypass).
Fire AgeGroupCatalogSeeded with { tenant_id, age_group_ids: [10 IDs], default_age_group_id: <Adult's ID>, seeded_by: 'tenant_provisioning', catalog_version: <int from data file> }.
Do NOT fire per-row AgeGroupCreatedNotification — the catalog-seed notification is a single roll-up event (silent by default since it's a system invariant, not a purchase).
jobs.note: Runs on the notifications queue because it's part of the tenant-onboarding path — its latency is user-visible (the tenant admin lands in a UI that expects the age-group dropdown to have entries).

jobs.purpose: Nightly at 03:30 UTC. Verifies the invariant `every (tenant, organization, sport_key) tuple with at least one active AgeGroup has EXACTLY ONE row with is_default=true`. Alerts on drift; when configured, auto-heals by promoting the oldest active row to default.
steps: SELECT tenant_id, organization_id, sport_key, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_default=true) AS default_count FROM age_groups WHERE deleted_at IS NULL GROUP BY tenant_id, organization_id, sport_key HAVING default_count != 1.
For each drifted tuple: log a telemetry counter (`academorix.age_group.default_invariant_drift.count`) + fire the alert per `config('age_group.reconciliation.alert_on_drift')`.
a. default_count = 0 AND >= 1 active row exists → promote the oldest active AgeGroup in the tuple to is_default=true.
b. default_count = 0 AND no active row exists → skip (the tuple is empty; no invariant to hold).
c. default_count > 1 → keep the newest is_default row; flip the older ones to is_default=false. Fire AgeGroupDefaultChanged for each demotion.
Write a reconciliation report row to `audit_events`.
jobs.note: Auto-heal is off by default even in production — surface + acknowledge before healing. Turn on when ops has built confidence in the drift patterns.

jobs.purpose: Daily at 04:30 UTC. Hard-deletes soft-deleted (archived) AgeGroup rows past `age_group.retention.archived_age_groups_retention_days` (default 1095 days — 3 years to match rolling youth-development cohort windows) IF no inbound FK row still references them.
steps: SELECT id, tenant_id FROM age_groups WHERE deleted_at IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at < NOW() - INTERVAL 'age_group.retention.archived_age_groups_retention_days days'.

For each: verify NO inbound row references (teams.age_group_id, event_divisions.age_group_id, team_registrations.eligibility_age_group_id). AthleteEnrollment.age_group_snapshot_id does NOT count — snapshots are self-contained.
When clear: hard-delete the row + fire AgeGroupDeleted with mode='hard'.
The audit row survives per minor-safeguarding retention (7 years — the audit outlives the AgeGroup).
jobs.note: Conservative — even at 1095 days, a single lingering inbound FK keeps the row alive indefinitely. Explicit tenant-admin action via `age-group:archive --force` bypasses the FK check (requires ops sign-off) but does NOT delete referring rows — it leaves them pointing at a now-archived AgeGroup which they survive for the retention window.
